issuesFound.title: Small base font size
issuesFound.explanation: The base font size is set to 12px on the html element. This makes the default text size for the entire page too small for many users, particularly those with visual impairments, and falls below the recommended 16px standard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 139: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative word-spacing value of -0.2em. This reduces the space between words, making text harder to distinguish and read for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative letter-spacing value of -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters can cause letters to overlap or blur together, significantly hindering readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 167: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have a line-height of 1. This fails to meet the minimum requirement of 1.5 for body text, which is necessary to ensure sufficient vertical space between lines for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from all anchor elements globally. Without underlines, links within blocks of text rely solely on color to be identified, which is a failure for users with color vision deficiency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none without defining a visible replacement style like a solid outline or box-shadow. This prevents keyboard users from knowing which element currently has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This implementation fails to visually hide the content, causing screen-reader-only text to appear on the screen for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable (#aaaaaa) has a contrast ratio of 2.32:1 against the white background (#ffffff). This is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: --colour-text-secondary: #6b6b6b;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle has a minimum height and width of 28px. This is below the 44x44px recommendation, making it difficult for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens to activate.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 511: min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive confirmation that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1063: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view-mode toggle buttons (grid/list) update CSS classes and local storage but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which mode is active. This prevents screen reader users from perceiving the current state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2405: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); (and set 'false' on the inactive sibling)`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid view mode is toggled between grid and list, the visual layout changes but no announcement is made to the screen reader. Users may be unaware that the content structure has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: `liveRegion.announce(`View changed to ${mode} mode`);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator updates the error message text and CSS classes but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Assistive technologies will not programmatically perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `clearField` function removes error classes and hides the error message but does not reset or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field previously marked invalid will continue to be announced as invalid even after being cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3165: `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization finds the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. Screen readers may not announce the button as a toggle until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation dropdown trigger uses the `isOpen` state to show/hide the sub-menu but does not communicate this state to screen readers. Without `aria-expanded`, non-visual users are not informed whether the menu is currently collapsed or expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons use the `value` prop to determine the active state but do not provide an `aria-pressed` attribute. This prevents screen reader users from identifying which billing cycle (Monthly or Annual) is selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1701: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`
  Line 1710: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has a visual error state when `invalid` is true, but it does not use the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen reader users will not be notified that the field contains an error during navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2035: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter submit button has an `isSubmitting` state that disables the button and shows a label change, but it lacks `aria-busy`. Assistive technologies will not announce that the process is currently active/busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2051: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation links use the `item.current` property to apply a visual "current" class but do not apply `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users cannot identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1058: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon in the navigation dropdown is purely decorative as the button already has a text label, but it is not hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1021: `<span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk used to visually indicate a required field is not hidden from screen readers. This results in the screen reader announcing "star" or "asterisk" which is redundant and potentially confusing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 864: ` *`

issuesFound.title: form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The search input in the header uses a `VisuallyHidden` label inside a `<label>` element, but the `<label>` itself is not correctly associated with the input because the `htmlFor` attribute is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1189: `<label id={labelId} htmlFor={id} className="site-search-form__label">`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transition and transform properties that cause motion, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disables this motion. This can cause discomfort or nausea for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card:hover selector uses transform and transition for motion without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may still experience unwanted animation, which can be distracting or harmful.

issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); causing spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable or reduce this motion. This can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-primary sets background-color: #5588bb and color: #ffffff, with a contrast ratio approximately 3:1, below the WCAG 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This makes button text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 407: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero image

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for FAQ button icon
issuesFound.explanation: .faq-item dt button::after has transform changes causing rotation, but the media query only disables transition, not the transform itself, so spatial movement still occurs instantly for reduced-motion users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 907: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.fix: Line 853: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2180: In the open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2200: In the close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Site search panel toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and siteSearch._close() functions show/hide the suggestion listbox but do not set aria-expanded on the search input (which has role="combobox"). Screen reader users will not know if the popup is open, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2870: In _open, add: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2879: In _close, add: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function updates CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed on the billing period toggle buttons. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button states.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know that the content has updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2470: In applyFilters, after the emit call, add:
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${s.activeCategory} products`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know the prices have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for form error indication.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up dropdowns but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on closed trigger buttons. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2330: In register, after setting panel hidden, add:
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Search input lacks initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function sets up the combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the search input. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the suggestion listbox, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2920: In init, after setting aria-haspopup, add:
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open/close lacks focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions do not manage keyboard focus. When the menu opens, focus should move to the first focusable element inside the drawer. When it closes, focus should return to the trigger. Keyboard and screen reader users cannot navigate the menu without this, violating WCAG 2.4.3.

issuesFound.title: FAQ search highlighting uses innerHTML without live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.highlightFaqTerms() function uses innerHTML to wrap search terms in <mark> elements but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know that terms have been highlighted, violating WCAG 4.1.3 for dynamic content changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4030: In highlightFaqTerms, after setting innerHTML, add:
liveRegion.announce(`Highlighted search terms in question: ${item.question}`);

issuesFound.title: Search results highlighter uses innerHTML without live region
issuesFound.explanation: The searchResultsHighlighter.highlight() function uses innerHTML to wrap matches in <mark> elements but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know that terms have been highlighted, violating WCAG 4.1.3 for dynamic content changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3130: In highlight, after calling _highlightTokensInElement, add:
liveRegion.announce(`Highlighted search terms in results`);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge and grid visibility but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the error state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', !result.valid); inside the _renderResult function.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after the debug log.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visible state of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2008: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 2016: ensure the toggle function correctly reflects the new state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _onToggleClick() and setPeriod() functions change the billing period state but do not update the aria-pressed attribute on the toggle switch element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the toggle's current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2508: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year');` after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`. Line 2516: in _syncToggleState(), add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, period === 'year');` after the class toggling.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2608: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult() function adds error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen reader users may not be alerted to validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3208: in the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`. Line 3216: in the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav and dropdownMenu init() functions do not set initial aria-expanded="false" on closed toggle buttons. Screen readers may not report the correct initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: in mobileNav.init(), after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`. Line 2100: in dropdownMenu.init(), after registering each trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _toggleEl click handler (_onToggleClick) is attached to a non-<button> element (likely a <div> or <span>) but no keydown handler for Enter/Space is provided. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: add `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });` after the click event listener.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult() function sets aria-invalid="true" for invalid fields but does not clear it (set to "false") when the field becomes valid. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3208: in the valid branch, ensure `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` is called (see Issue 5 fix).

fpTitles: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn:focus-visible` removes the outline (`outline: none`) but does not supply any other visible focus cue, leaving button focus invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` with no alternative visual focus indicator, breaking keyboard navigation for the filter tabs.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible` removes the outline without providing any other visible focus styling, making the accordion controls invisible when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible` removes the outline and does not add any alternative visual cue, leaving the toggle buttons without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links a:focus-visible` removes the outline without any replacement, so screen‑reader users cannot see which social link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 × 44 px touch target, making it hard for finger users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;` which is below the 44 px minimum, reducing tap accuracy.

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto;` which does **not** hide the element from the accessibility tree. It also fails to provide the required 1 px × 1 px clipping technique, effectively making the content visible to AT but not visually hidden correctly.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links. No non‑colour visual differentiator (e.g., border‑bottom, background highlight) is provided for the normal state, so users must rely on colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img:hover` applies a `transform` transition (rotateY, rotateX) that creates motion. No corresponding rule inside `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` disables or reduces this animation, violating users who request reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-3px)` with a transition, creating motion. No reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card:hover` animates `transform: translateY(-2px)` and a box‑shadow transition. No reduced‑motion override is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` includes `transform: translateY(-1px)` with a transition, creating motion. No reduced‑motion media query disables this effect.

issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on inset focus‑ring utility
issuesFound.explanation: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible` removes the outline and only changes the offset, providing no visible focus cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on brand focus‑ring utility
issuesFound.explanation: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible` removes the outline and only sets an outline colour, which does not render because the outline style is none. No visible focus indicator remains.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; outline-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-brand-primary); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on base button
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` sets `min-height: 32px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended minimum touch target of 44 × 44 px, making it difficult for finger users to activate buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn { min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a very small tap area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on large button variant
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-lg` sets `min-height: 36px`, still below the 44 px minimum target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 390: `.btn-lg { min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon‑only button
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` sets `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;` which is well under the 44 px recommendation, making icon‑only buttons hard to tap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on mobile navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: `.nav-toggle` defines `min-height: 28px; min-width: 28px;` which is below the 44 px minimum, reducing tapability on small screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-toggle button` sets `min-height: 28px;` which is below the recommended 44 px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on social‑link anchors
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links a` has `min-height: 28px;` which does not meet the 44 px minimum target area for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.social-links a { min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on hero CTA button hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` creating motion, but there is no corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable or simplify this animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the movement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 460: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); /* add reduced‑motion guard */ }`  
  (Add a matching rule inside the reduced‑motion media query, e.g.: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on button active state
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn:active` applies `transform: translateY(1px);` which creates motion on activation, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, affecting users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 350: `.btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); /* add reduced‑motion guard */ }`  
  (Add inside reduced‑motion media query: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`)

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;`, which compresses spacing between words and can make text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: `word-spacing: normal; /* remove negative word‑spacing */`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other non‑colour visual differentiator (such as border‑bottom, background, or font‑weight change) is provided. Users must rely on colour alone to identify links, which may not be sufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `a { text-decoration: none; /* add alternative visual cue, e.g.: border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; */ }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu`, each trigger button receives `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-controls`, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` when the dropdown panel is opened or closed. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Also set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on each trigger during initialization.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed and given `aria-controls` and `aria-labelledby`, but the `open(item)` and `close(item)` functions never set or clear the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Users of assistive technology cannot tell which FAQ item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Initialise each trigger with `aria-expanded="false"`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` and made focusable, but the script never updates the `aria‑pressed` state when the user switches between monthly and annual billing. This violates the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick()` (or after changing period) call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`. Also set an initial `aria-pressed="false"` on page load.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` when the drawer opens or `aria‑expanded="false"` when it closes, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `open()` and `close()` functions (around line 1170), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true')` on open and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` on close.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button does not have an `aria‑controls` reference to the navigation drawer element, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_drawer` in `init()`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'))`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close dropdown panels are given `aria‑haspopup` but the script never updates `aria‑expanded` to reflect the open/closed state, breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance(inst)` set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance(inst)` set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the dropdown triggers do not have `aria‑expanded` defined, so screen readers assume an unknown state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register(trigger, panel)` after setting `aria‑haspopup`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` if the attribute is absent.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons control the visibility of answer panels but never toggle `aria‑expanded`, leaving the state undisclosed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` set `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` set `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, FAQ triggers have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so their collapsed state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix:
In `parse(el, cfg)` after creating `trigger`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` unless the item is pre‑opened.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch billing periods use visual classes to indicate selection but never set `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState(period)` after toggling the active class, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(isActive))` for each period button.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` but its `aria‑pressed` state is never changed when the user toggles it, breaking the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()` (or `_onToggleKeydown`) after changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`.

---

issuesFound.title: `srUtils.markInvalid` does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The helper used by inline validation adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the invalid input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` add `setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true')` before calling `addDescribedBy`. Also ensure the corresponding “valid” path clears the attribute.

issuesFound.explanation: Each <FormGroup> renders a <label> without an htmlFor attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot associate the label with its input. This affects all form fields that use <FormGroup>.
issuesFound.fix:
Add htmlFor={htmlFor} to the <label> element.  
Line 352: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`  
Line 398: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`  
Line 452: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`  
Line 506: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`  
Line 560: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`  
Line 614: `<label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}>`

issuesFound.title: Input missing accessible label (HTML <label not associated)
issuesFound.explanation: Each <TextInput> or <Textarea> has an id but no <label linked via htmlFor, nor aria‑label/aria‑labelledby, leaving the control unnamed for assistive technology. This impacts all form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the corresponding <FormGroup> provides htmlFor matching the input id (and the <label> has htmlFor). The <FormGroup> fix above resolves this.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the <TextInput> or <Textarea> receives a custom invalid prop, which renders as a non‑standard invalid attribute instead of aria‑invalid, so screen readers are not notified of the error state. Affects all fields with validation.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass aria-invalid={invalid} instead of invalid.  
Line 360: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" aria-invalid={invalid} … />`  
Line 460: `<TextInput id="contact-name" aria-invalid={invalid} … />`  
Line 514: `<TextInput id="contact-email" aria-invalid={invalid} … />`  
Line 568: `<TextInput id="contact-subject" aria-invalid={invalid} … />`  
Line 622: `<Textarea id="contact-message" aria-invalid={invalid} … />`

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: When an error message is rendered by <ErrorMessage>, the associated input does not reference it with aria-describedby, so the error text is not announced. Affects all fields with possible errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass aria-describedby with the error id to the input.  
Line 368: `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"`  
Line 468: `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"`  
Line 522: `aria-describedby="contact-email-error"`  
Line 576: `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"`  
Line 630: `aria-describedby="contact-message-error"`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑expanded attribute (NavDropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger <button> controls the submenu via aria-controls but does not indicate its expanded state with aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button.  
Line 274: `<button … aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} …>`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑expanded attribute (Mobile navigation toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle <button> controls the off‑canvas panel via aria-controls but lacks aria‑expanded, so screen readers cannot tell if the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button.  
Line 642: `<button … aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} …>`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute (Hero video play/pause)
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause <Button> toggles video playback but does not expose its pressed state via aria‑pressed, making the current state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-pressed={isPlaying} to the button.  
Line 210: `<Button … aria-pressed={isPlaying} …>`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute (Filter tabs)
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter‑tab <button> indicates selection via styling but does not expose aria‑pressed, leaving the state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-pressed={activeFilter===tab.value} to each button.  
Line 1150: `<button … aria-pressed={activeFilter===tab.value} …>`

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only interactive element missing accessible name (Mobile navigation close button)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> used to close the mobile menu contains only an “✕” icon and lacks aria‑label, so screen readers have no name for the control.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide an aria-label (e.g., "Close navigation menu").

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only interactive element missing accessible name (Theme toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> that cycles themes shows only an emoji and has no aria‑label, making it unnamed for AT.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only interactive element missing accessible name (Testimonials carousel previous/next buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel navigation <IconButton> elements contain only “‹”/“›” icons and lack aria‑label, so AT cannot identify their purpose.

issuesFound.title: Div with onClick without role or keyboard support (Mobile navigation backdrop)
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div> captures click events to close the menu but is not focusable nor announced as a button, preventing keyboard users from activating it.

Line 632: `<button className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation backdrop" />`

issuesFound.title: Div with onClick without role or keyboard support (Modal overlay)
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div> closes the dialog on click but is not focusable nor announced as a button, so keyboard users cannot dismiss the modal by clicking the overlay.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `on()` function (lines 1063–1100) attaches click event listeners to DOM elements but does not provide matching keyboard handlers (keydown/keyup) for Enter and Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` styled as buttons) cannot be activated via keyboard.

fpTitles: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module (lines 10325–10599) binds a non-native interactive element (the upload zone) with `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` but only attaches a `click` event listener. Keyboard users who press Enter or Space on this element will not trigger the intended action, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.1.1 Keyboard. This affects users who rely on keyboard navigation, including screen reader users and those with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module (lines 4900–5249) contains a toggle function (`toggle()` at line 4950) that toggles the mobile navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen readers will not announce the open/closed state of the drawer, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. This affects screen reader users who rely on ARIA attributes to understand interactive element states.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Root font-size reduces body text below readability threshold

issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~395: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition, creating a 3D rotation effect on hover. The existing prefers-reduced-motion media query block is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~810: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can make reading difficult for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word spacing reduces legibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~285: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) for body text. This requires users to zoom more to read comfortably and may cause text spacing issues when users override styles.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~245: html { ... font-size: 100%; ... }

issuesFound.title: Line height too tight on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is well below the recommended 1.5. This tight spacing makes text difficult to read, especially for users with low vision or reading disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~315: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1), making hints hard to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1215: .input-hint { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on required field markers
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker selector uses colour-error (#ee9999) on white, giving a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1), making the required indicator difficult to see.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~415: .required-marker { color: var(--colour-error-dark); margin-left: var(--space-0-5); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); font-size: 1em; line-height: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "not included" indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table cells for "Not included" use colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) on white or neutral-50 backgrounds, resulting in approximately 1.9:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components), making status indicators illegible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1140: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The input::placeholder selector uses colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on white, giving approximately 3.3:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1), making placeholder instructions hard to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~260: input::placeholder, textarea::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); opacity: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses white at 30% opacity (rgba(255,255,255,0.3)) on a blue-purple gradient background (~#5588bb), resulting in contrast well below 4.5:1. Users cannot read the statistic descriptions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1045: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .input-hint uses white at 65% opacity on a coloured gradient background, resulting in contrast below 4.5:1. Hint text is illegible against the brand colours.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1220: .newsletter-section .input-hint { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector relies on colour alone to distinguish links from text, with no underline, border-bottom, or background change on hover (only colour change). Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot identify these as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~745: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector removes underlines and only changes colour on hover. Without additional visual cues like underline or border, colourblind users cannot distinguish these links from plain text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1285: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector relies solely on colour to indicate links, with no underline or other non-colour visual differentiator. Users who cannot perceive the colour change will not recognize these as interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1325: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a high-contrast alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode lose focus visibility entirely.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when this button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Feature card links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Links within feature cards remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which tab button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which toggle button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The table wrapper removes outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable region has focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which accordion button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes have focus.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.

issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has a minimum height and width of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have explicit width and height of 20px, below the minimum 24px touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty selecting these checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links.

issuesFound.title: Social links touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible to all users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Feature card transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Product card transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The product card includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Stat item transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The stat item includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Danger button insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses background-color #ee9999 (light red) with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.47:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter section insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses a gradient background ending in #bb88ee (light purple) with white text, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor element selector removes text underlines without providing an alternative visual indicator such as border-bottom or background-color change on hover. This makes it difficult for users to identify links within text, especially those with colour vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size to 12px, which sets the root em unit to below 16px. Body text at 1rem will be 12px, which is smaller than the recommended minimum for readable text and may cause readability issues for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements set line-height to 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 minimum for readable text. This tight spacing can cause readability issues for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not set aria-expanded when opening or closing panels, and the attribute is not initialized at startup. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2280: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2295: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`
  Line 2350: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to true (though it is correctly set to false on close). This leaves screen reader users unaware that the answer panel is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Category filter tab buttons use visual styling to show the active filter but do not set aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot identify which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility removes error descriptions but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute from the field, leaving it incorrectly marked as invalid for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: Insert `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` immediately after the `if (refs.length)` block (before the closing brace of the function).

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and visibility changes are not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware that the grid has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);` immediately after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied'...`

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, the price values update visually but are not announced to screen reader users, who may miss the change in pricing information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or appropriate message for the period) immediately after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls but the action is not announced to screen reader users, who may not realize the page position has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` immediately after `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });` (or after the scrollToY call).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded or collapsed state of the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` before the emit call. Line 2350 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle functions open and close the panel but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger), violating the ARIA disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2460 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions animate the panel and update state but omit updating the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (item.trigger).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3040 (in close function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has role="combobox" but the _open() and _close() functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2720 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 2730 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not update aria-pressed on billing period toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as toggle buttons but the _syncToggleState function does not update aria-pressed to indicate the active state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2890 (in _syncToggleState function): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility marks fields as invalid by linking error messages via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so assistive technologies do not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200 (in markInvalid function): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the utility removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2210 (in markValid function): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, leaving screen readers unaware that the button controls an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2310 (in init function): After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered, violating the requirement that toggle buttons indicate their initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430 (in register function): After `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input with role="combobox" is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", so screen readers do not know the listbox is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750 (in init function): After `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no live region announcement is made, so screen reader users are not informed of the result count or that filtering completed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2560 (in filterAnimation.run callback): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the new prices or billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2880 (in setPeriod function): Before `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close do not announce state changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users may not realize the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020 (in open function): Before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`. Line 3040 (in close function): Before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger buttons
faqAccordion open/close do not announce state changes to screen readers

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function displays the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1784: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the `_lockScroll();` call and before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1802: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the `_unlockScroll();` call and before the `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();` line.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, violating the requirement that the initial state of expandable widgets must be programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the `_initialised = true;` line and before the event listener attachments.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals a dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1972: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides a dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu is now closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1986: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (the close function sets it to false, creating an asymmetry). Screen readers will incorrectly report the item as collapsed while it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3241: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox is displayed but aria-expanded remains unset on the input element, so screen readers do not announce that the listbox is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2641: Insert `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox is hidden but aria-expanded is not set to "false" on the input element, so screen readers may still believe the listbox is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2652: Insert `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init() missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is initialised as a combobox but lacks aria-expanded="false", preventing screen readers from determining the initial collapsed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2724: Insert `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing._updatePlanCard() dynamic price change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price text is updated visually and via aria-label, but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users navigating the page non-sequentially will not hear that prices have changed across all plan cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2860: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Prices updated to ${priceFormatter.periodLabel(period)} billing');` immediately after the price display update logic (after the animateNumber onComplete or after the textContent assignment).

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge() dynamic count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge is updated visually when filters are applied, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, so users do not know how many results match their current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2435: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` immediately after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no status message is sent to the live region. Screen reader users do not receive confirmation that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1784: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` before the `emit()` call. Line 1802: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no status message is sent to the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the menu has appeared or disappeared, especially if focus did not move into the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1972: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` before the `emit()` call. Line 1986: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before the `emit()` call.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
productFilter._updateCountBadge() dynamic count not announced
dropdownMenu open/close state not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page (desktop navigation, footer navigation), screen reader users cannot distinguish the purpose of this landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 405: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative star spans not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★ and ☆) inside the rating component are exposed to screen readers as text content, causing redundant announcements since the parent already has an aria-label with the rating value. Each span containing a star character needs aria-hidden="true".

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state

issuesFound.fix: Line 525: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={...}>
