issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but is missing aria-modal="true". Without aria-modal, screen readers may announce content outside the dialog, confusing users about which content is interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 505: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: aria-modal="true" is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". aria-modal must be on the dialog element itself to properly signal modality to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1175: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 1177: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark for skip links does not have an accessible name. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish between them when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation landmark does not have an accessible name. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish between them when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 468: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation body does not have an accessible name. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish between them when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: Spinner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative spinner ring span is not hidden from assistive technology. The parent element already has role="status" and an accessible label, so the decorative ring should be aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative button spinner span is not hidden from assistive technology. The loading state is already announced via VisuallyHidden, so the decorative spinner should be aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 309: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters are decorative since the parent span already has aria-label conveying the rating. The stars should be hidden from assistive technology to prevent redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: Complex formatted values like "40,000+" may be announced inconsistently by screen readers. The valueLabel property provides a screen-reader-friendly version but is not used.
issuesFound.fix: Line 691:

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active/current page link uses only CSS class to indicate the current state. Without aria-current="page", screen reader users cannot identify which navigation item represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active/current page link in mobile navigation uses only CSS class to indicate the current state. Without aria-current="page", screen reader users cannot identify which navigation item represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 571: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), aria-busy is not communicated to assistive technology. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 297: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>
